imports: from ai_dev_browser.core.browser import browser_list, browser_start, browser_stop
from ai_dev_browser.core.connection import BrowserClient, connect_browser, get_active_tab
from ai_dev_browser.core.port import find_debug_chromes, find_workspace_chromes, is_port_in_use

fixtures.name: cleanup_test_chromes
fixtures.doc: Track and cleanup Chromes started during each test

scenarios.name: workspace_tag_in_chrome_cmdline
scenarios.title: Verify workspace tag appears in Chrome's command line
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Read cmdline → Verify workspace tag matches cwd
scenarios.user_problem: When multiple AI agents run on the same machine, each Chrome must be tagged with the owning workspace so they don't steal each other's instances

scenarios.name: workspace_slug_deterministic_and_unique
scenarios.title: Workspace slug is deterministic and different paths produce different slugs
scenarios.workflow: Generate slug for cwd → Generate slug for different path → Verify they differ but cwd slug is stable
scenarios.user_problem: Profile directories must be deterministic (same path always maps to same profile) and collision-free (different paths get different profiles)

scenarios.name: workspace_profile_dir_isolation
scenarios.title: Different workspaces get different profile directories
scenarios.workflow: Get profile dir for workspace A → Get profile dir for workspace B → Verify they differ
scenarios.user_problem: Each AI agent workspace must have isolated Chrome profiles so login state doesn't leak between projects

scenarios.name: workspace_chrome_filtering
scenarios.title: find_workspace_chromes only returns Chromes from current workspace
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Verify find_workspace_chromes finds it → Verify find_debug_chromes shows workspace
scenarios.user_problem: Auto-detection must only return Chromes belonging to the current workspace, not Chromes from other agents

assertions: any(p == started_port for p, _pid in ws_chromes)
any(p == started_port and ws is not None for p, _pid, ws in all_chromes)

scenarios.name: browser_list_workspace_filtering
scenarios.title: browser_list defaults to current workspace, all_workspaces shows all
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → List (default) → Verify visible → List (all_workspaces) → Verify workspace field
scenarios.user_problem: AI agent should see only its own browsers by default, but can see all with explicit flag

assertions: any(b['port'] == started_port for b in filtered_list['browsers'])
any(b['port'] == started_port for b in full_list['browsers'])

scenarios.name: graceful_shutdown_preserves_method
scenarios.title: browser_stop uses graceful CDP shutdown and reports method
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Stop gracefully → Verify method=graceful and port freed
scenarios.user_problem: Graceful shutdown via CDP Browser.close() flushes cookies to profile SQLite; force-kill loses them

scenarios.name: graceful_stop_all_workspaces
scenarios.title: browser_stop(stop_all=True) stops all Chromes gracefully
scenarios.workflow: Start two Chromes → Stop all → Verify both stopped → Verify ports freed
scenarios.user_problem: AI agent needs to clean up all Chrome instances at end of session

scenarios.name: workspace_reuse_only_own_chrome
scenarios.title: browser_start reuse only finds Chromes in current workspace
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Verify reuse finds it → Stop → Start again → Verify new Chrome created
scenarios.user_problem: Reuse strategy must not accidentally attach to another agent's Chrome

scenarios.name: reuse_across_profiles_in_same_workspace
scenarios.title: Default reuse finds idle Chrome even with different profile name
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome (profile A) → Start Chrome (profile B, reuse=any) → Verify reused same port
scenarios.user_problem: Within the same workspace, default reuse strategy should find any idle Chrome before creating a new one

scenarios.name: reuse_none_same_profile_still_detects
scenarios.title: reuse='none' skips reuse scan but profile check catches existing Chrome
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Start again with reuse=none + same profile → Verify still reused via profile detection
scenarios.user_problem: Even when reuse scanning is disabled, starting Chrome with an already-active profile must reuse it to avoid Chrome conflicts

scenarios.name: reuse_none_different_profile_new_chrome
scenarios.title: reuse='none' + different profile = truly new Chrome
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome (profile A, reuse=none) → Start Chrome (profile B, reuse=none) → Verify different ports
scenarios.user_problem: With reuse disabled and different profiles, each gets its own Chrome instance

scenarios.name: stop_frees_port
scenarios.title: Stopping a browser frees its debug port
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Verify port in use → Stop → Verify port freed
scenarios.user_problem: Ports must be released after stop so they can be reused by future Chrome instances

scenarios.name: restart_after_stop
scenarios.title: Can restart Chrome with same profile after stopping
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Stop → Start again → Verify fresh (not reused)
scenarios.user_problem: After stopping a browser, starting again with the same profile should launch a new instance

scenarios.name: list_shows_port_and_pid
scenarios.title: browser_list includes port and pid for each browser
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → List → Verify port and pid present
scenarios.user_problem: Listing must show identifying info (port, pid) for each browser instance

assertions: any(b['port'] == port and 'pid' in b for b in listing['browsers'])

scenarios.name: connection_reuse_same_port
scenarios.title: Multiple connect_browser() calls reuse the same BrowserClient
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Connect twice → Verify same instance → Execute JS
scenarios.user_problem: Without connection reuse, multiple connect_browser() calls leak WebSockets and exhaust Chrome's CDP slots

steps.description: Execute JS to verify connection works

scenarios.name: close_then_reconnect_fresh_instance
scenarios.title: After close(), reconnecting creates a fresh BrowserClient
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Connect → Close → Reconnect → Verify different instance → JS works
scenarios.user_problem: After explicitly closing a connection, the next connect must create a fresh instance rather than returning a dead cached one

scenarios.name: context_manager_clears_cache
scenarios.title: async with connect_browser() cleans up cache on exit
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → async with connect_browser → JS works → Exit → Cache cleared
scenarios.user_problem: Context manager must remove the cached BrowserClient so stale connections don't accumulate

steps.description: Use async context manager, verify JS works, then verify cache cleared on exit
raw_code: async with await connect_browser(port=port) as browser:
# Cache should be cleared after context manager exit

scenarios.name: tab_reconnects_after_ws_disconnect
scenarios.title: Tab auto-reconnects when its WebSocket dies
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Connect → JS works → Kill WS → JS works again (auto-reconnect)
scenarios.user_problem: WebSocket connections can drop (idle timeout, network hiccup); tab must transparently reconnect

steps.description: Simulate WebSocket dying and verify auto-reconnect
raw_code: # Next call should auto-reconnect and succeed

scenarios.name: screenshot_after_ws_reconnect
scenarios.title: Screenshot works after tab WebSocket reconnects
scenarios.workflow: Start Chrome → Screenshot → Kill WS → Screenshot again (Page.enable re-enabled)
scenarios.user_problem: Page.captureScreenshot requires Page.enable() which must be re-sent after WebSocket reconnection

steps.description: Kill WS, screenshot again, cleanup files
raw_code: # Screenshot after reconnect — Page domain must be re-enabled
